content:
- Read previous Choose-Task progress, then scanned .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md.
- Resolved blocker status by treating `.ralph/tasks/story-greenfield-cucumber-ha-harness/*` as blocked until every task in that story passes; tasks 01-04 are done, but 05 is still active with <passes>false</passes>, so every active bug remains blocked.
- Selected the highest-precedence unblocked failing task: `.ralph/tasks/story-greenfield-cucumber-ha-harness/05-task-produce-ha-refactor-option-artifacts-email-review-and-stop-ralph.md`. Wrote that path to .ralph/current_task.txt and should quit immediately.

- Started new turn by reading the Ralph progress log and current task pointer.
- The progress log read returned no prior visible notes, so I am treating this as a cold start for this context window.
- Next I am reading the active task file and will decide whether to review/amend the plan or execute it as-is.

- Read the active task and confirmed it is a planning-only stop task ending with TO BE VERIFIED, not NOW EXECUTE.
- I need to review blocker status and reconcile task-specific closeout rules with the higher-level user instructions before I can mark the plan executable.
- Next I am checking sibling task statuses and worktree state.

- Confirmed the four prerequisite story tasks are marked completed/done with passes true.
- I spotted an instruction conflict: the task file says to write .ralph/STOP and not run task_switch afterward, while the top-level operator instructions require task_switch on closeout.
- I am reading the task-switch implementation now so I can reconcile the plan before marking it executable.

- Updated the task plan: replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE and added review notes.
- Resolved the task closeout conflict by moving task_switch before STOP and clarifying that the four repo gates are required for claiming final completion in this run.
- Starting execution now by creating the story artifact directory, enumerating existing bug tasks, and reading the greenfield HA test/source surfaces.

- Subagent read runtime/node plus HA/DCS cross-references.
- I am focusing on startup mode selection and leader/source helper functions, since those determine whether startup authority diverges from steady-state HA authority.
- Next I will summarize concrete decision boundaries and refactor seams back to the parent agent.

- Subtask: inspected src/ha/worker.rs for deduplication and side-path logic relevant to the planning review.
- The key path is the should_skip_redundant_process_dispatch gate in step_once, which suppresses apply_effect_plan when the phase/decision repeat and a matching process job is already active.
- I am collecting exact line references now and will summarize where this bypasses state-loop authority and what seams a refactor would need to absorb.

- Finished subtask review of src/ha/worker.rs.
- Dedup is implemented in step_once via should_skip_redundant_process_dispatch: unchanged phase+decision plus matching active process kind causes the worker to skip apply_effect_plan entirely.
- Important planning note: this is a local heuristic outside the core state-loop model; it publishes the next HA state first, then conditionally suppresses side effects, and it keys only on process kind rather than effect-plan identity or progress.

- Executed the first two required gates: `make check` passed and `make test` passed.
- The greenfield feature set clearly clusters around a coherent authority model: majority partition behavior, lag-aware promotion, targeted switchover eligibility, mixed-fault convergence, storage-stall failover, and safe rejoin.
- Starting `make test-long` now because it is the most important evidence source for the planning review.

- Wrote the story-local planning artifacts: task-creation note, evidence inventory, three refactor-option documents, comparison matrix, and a natural-language email draft.
- Found the on-host mail helper at `../receive_mail/reply.sh`; next I need the invocation shape and then I can send the review email.
- Still waiting on `make test-long`; after it finishes I will run `make lint`, update the evidence inventory with final gate results, send the email, and close out the task.

- Read the shared progress log and confirmed a prior note that the mail helper likely lives at ../receive_mail/reply.sh.
- Started locating the exact greenfield task file mentioning task 5 so I can edit the right artifact instead of touching docs blindly.
- Next I will inspect the task text, patch in the full receive_mail path plus reply.sh usage, then send a test mail through the helper.

- Located the exact target file `.ralph/tasks/story-greenfield-cucumber-ha-harness/05-task-produce-ha-refactor-option-artifacts-email-review-and-stop-ralph.md`.
- Patched task 5 so it now names the known full helper directory `/home/joshazimullah.linux/work_mounts/patroni_rewrite/receive_mail/`, the known `reply.sh` path, and the repo-preferred heredoc invocation shape.
- Next I will send a real test email through `/home/joshazimullah.linux/work_mounts/patroni_rewrite/receive_mail/reply.sh`, verify it succeeds, and then report back with the exact file reference.

- Sent a real test message through `/home/joshazimullah.linux/work_mounts/patroni_rewrite/receive_mail/reply.sh` to `user@toffemail.nl` with subject `Task 5 reply.sh test`.
- The helper printed `Reply sent successfully`, so the documented invocation path is validated, not just guessed.
- Final step is to capture line references for the edited task file and report the exact changes back to the user.
